All of the fantasy baseball news, notes, and top performances you NEED to know about 🧵👇 https://t.co/hZcLi7IZqN
1. Ketel Marte continued his strong play Wednesday, going 2-for-4 with a double, an RBI, two stolen bases and a run scored in a 6-4 loss to the Giants.
2. Freddie Freeman homered, swiped a bag and drew a walk in Wednesday's 7-1 loss to the Athletics, going 1-for-2 on the day.
3. Shea Langeliers went 2-for-5 with a solo home run Wednesday as the Athletics topped the Dodgers 7-1.
4. Zack Wheeler exited Wednesday's start against the Pirates without a decision after allowing four runs on nine hits across 4.2 innings, though he did punch out 10 batters.
5. Paul Skenes fell to 6-8 on Wednesday after a rough outing against the Phillies, surrendering eight runs (seven earned) on six hits and two walks across four innings while striking out five.
6. Junior Caminero delivered the only offense in Wednesday's 4-0 win over the Royals, going 1-for-4 with a two-run homer.
7. James Wood was a force in Washington's 10-2 rout of the Red Sox on Wednesday, going 2-for-4 with two walks, a three-run homer and two runs scored.
8. Mookie Betts was scratched from Wednesday's lineup against the Athletics due to wrist soreness, making him a name to monitor for fantasy managers.
9. Pete Crow-Armstrong shined in the Cubs' 23-3 demolition of the Padres on Wednesday, going 2-for-4 with a three-run homer, a stolen base and two runs scored.
10. Brent Rooker's season is over after the outfielder was diagnosed with a cartilage tear in his left knee that will require surgery.
